title: Calculating z-scores
description: Find the z-score of a particular measurement given the mean and standard deviation.

assessment_items.question_content:
The grades on a language midterm at Santa Rita are roughly symmetric with $\mu = 70$ and $\sigma = 5.5$. 
 
Ishaan scored $72$ on the exam.

 **Find the z-score for Ishaan's exam grade. Round to two decimal places.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: A z-score is defined as the number of standard deviations a specific point is away from the mean.
$\large{\quad z \quad = \quad \dfrac{72 - \pink{70}}{\green{5.5}}}$

$\large{\quad z \quad \approx \quad 0.36}$

  The z-score is $0.36$. In other words, Ishaan's score was $0.36$ standard deviations above the mean.

assessment_items.question_content:
The grades on a math midterm at Loyola are roughly symmetric with $\mu = 68$ and $\sigma = 5.5$.

 **Find the z-score for Jessica's exam grade. Round to two decimal places.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\large{\quad z \quad = \quad \dfrac{66 - \pink{68}}{\green{5.5}}}$

$\large{\quad z \quad \approx \quad -0.36}$

  The z-score is $-0.36$. In other words, Jessica's score was $0.36$ standard deviations below the mean.
